summary: Cathy O'Neil invites readers to join Alt Banking, her Occupy Wall Street working group that meets weekly at Columbia University. The group has built an informative website, hosts notable speakers including Neil Barofsky and Sheila Bair, and published a book called 'Occupy Finance.' They're launching a new multimedia project exploring the intersection of three topics (visualized as a Venn diagram), potentially culminating in a conference. The post emphasizes both the substantive work and the community aspect of the group.
key_points: Alt Banking meets every Sunday at Columbia and welcomes new members, including visitors passing through New York
The group has hosted impressive speakers from finance and activism, with more events scheduled
Their book 'Occupy Finance' sold out its first printing and is available as a free PDF
A new multimedia project is in development, potentially combining book, video, and animated content for a September rollout
The group combines serious financial activism with genuine community and social connection
money_quotes: I'm really proud of my Occupy group, Alt Banking.
We continue to meet every Sunday at Columbia and we welcome new members.
I'd say it's a great group to know about for the speaker list alone.
We wrote a pretty cool book called Occupy Finance, and we recently got a second printing made since the first printing went so quickly.
I also wanted to mention that we have a blast every week.
key_insight: Sustained financial reform activism requires combining serious intellectual work with welcoming community to keep people engaged over the long term.
